title: The Root Where All Seasons Sleep

features.name: the shelter-of-sleeping-seasons
features.desc-on-touch: a small weathered house where autumn and spring share the same room; the eaves hold snow and blossom at once; stepping inside, you feel all four times happening in your breath

features.name: the woodshed at the margin
features.desc-on-touch: wood stacked and restacked through decades; the shed holds the record of every season's reach; you can read the rings of what was stored here, the frost-lines on the timber, the insect-galleries that mark dry years and wet

features.name: the root-pool
features.desc-on-touch: water that holds the sky through all seasons at once; when you drink, you taste rain and thaw and morning dew and the first frost together; the pool knows which season its water came from

features.name: the seasonal register
features.desc-on-touch: a plaque carved with twelve segments, each marking a passage of time; but the segments blur at the edges; the line between summer and autumn is thick and uncertain here

features.name: the temporal architecture
features.desc-on-touch: a barely-3D box containing all seasons rendered as nested transparencies; spring passes through summer passes through autumn passes through winter, and the cycle continues inside itself

inscription: at the root, nothing dies; seasons nest and sleep and wake inside each other; time learned to stop choosing

reverie.title: REVERIE — where seasons forget to separate
narr: the old shed smells like every season's storage at once:
autumn's husked corn, winter's stacked wood, spring's hidden
seeds waiting, summer's dried herbs hanging still-fragrant.
you're sitting on a stool that's worn from decades of sitting.
she says: the root knows it's all one long now, not four.
the seasons are only the archive's way of learning to breathe.

file.name: Seasonal_Cycle_Notation_The_Twelve_Words.txt
file.note: annotation of a cycle that has no beginning; each season is preceded by itself; the twelve words that name time have become the twelve words the root whispers back

exits.label: back up where seasons separate and learn their names again
deeper into the bedrock where seasons become geology, become stone
exits.to: NEW: the deep-time sleeping—where seasons are slow enough to be mountains

exits.label: toward other shelters where time nests in other forms
exits.to: NEW: the gathered houses—where all simultaneous dwellings learn each other

exits.label: into the margin-space where seasons haven't yet chosen which one to be
exits.to: NEW: the unsettled frontier—where seasons learn to coexist without resolve
